title: phelipanche ramosa
intro: Phelipanche ramosa, also known as broomrape, is a parasitic plant that derives its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of other plants. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Phelipanche ramosa include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and exhibiting potential anticancer properties. Phelipanche ramosa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder for use in herbal remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
